   327→    def _copy_and_notify(self, text: str) -> None:
   328→        """Copy text to clipboard and show notification.
   329→
   330→        Args:
   331→            text: Text to copy to clipboard
   332→        """
   333→        if self._clipboard is not None:
   334→            self._clipboard.copy(text)
   335→        # Show brief notification via app's notify method
   336→        self.notify(f"Copied: {text}", timeout=2)
   337→
   338→    def action_copy_checkout(self) -> None:
   339→        """Copy local checkout command to clipboard."""
   340→        if self._row.worktree_branch is None:
   341→            self.notify(
   342→                "No branch associated with this plan is checked out in a local worktree",
   343→                severity="warning",
   344→            )
   345→            return
   346→        cmd = f"erk br co {self._row.worktree_branch}"
   347→        self._copy_and_notify(cmd)
   348→
   349→    def action_copy_pr_checkout(self) -> None:
   350→        """Copy PR checkout command to clipboard."""
   351→        text = get_copy_text("copy_pr_checkout", self._row, self._view_mode)
   352→        if text is not None:
   353→            self._copy_and_notify(text)
   354→
   355→    def action_copy_prepare(self) -> None:
   356→        """Copy basic prepare command to clipboard."""
   357→        cmd = f"erk br co --for-plan {self._row.plan_id}"
   358→        self._copy_and_notify(cmd)
   359→
   360→    def action_copy_prepare_activate(self) -> None:
   361→        """Copy one-liner to prepare worktree and start implementation."""
   362→        cmd = (
   363→            f'source "$(erk br co --for-plan {self._row.plan_id} --script)"'
   364→            " && erk implement --dangerous"
   365→        )
   366→        self._copy_and_notify(cmd)
   367→
   368→    def action_copy_implement_local(self) -> None:
   369→        """Copy one-liner to checkout PR and start local implementation."""
   370→        if self._row.pr_number is not None:
   371→            cmd = (
   372→                f'source "$(erk pr checkout {self._row.pr_number} --script)"'
   373→                " && erk implement --dangerous"
   374→            )
   375→            self._copy_and_notify(cmd)
   376→
   377→    def action_copy_dispatch(self) -> None:
   378→        """Copy dispatch command to clipboard."""
   379→        cmd = f"erk pr dispatch {self._row.plan_id}"
   380→        self._copy_and_notify(cmd)
   381→
   382→    def action_rebase_remote(self) -> None:
   383→        """Launch remote rebase workflow."""
   384→        from erk.tui.app import ErkDashApp
   385→
   386→        if self._row.pr_number is None or self._repo_root is None:
   387→            return
   388→        self.dismiss()
   389→        if isinstance(self.app, ErkDashApp):
   390→            op_id = f"rebase-pr-{self._row.pr_number}"
   391→            self.app.notify(f"Dispatching rebase for PR #{self._row.pr_number}...")
   392→            self.app._start_operation(
   393→                op_id=op_id,
   394→                label=f"Dispatching rebase for PR #{self._row.pr_number}...",
   395→            )
   396→            self.app._rebase_remote_async(op_id, self._row.pr_number)
   397→
   398→    def action_copy_output_logs(self) -> None:
   399→        """Copy command output logs to clipboard."""
   400→        if self._output_panel is None:
   401→            return
   402→        if not self._output_panel.is_completed:
   403→            return
   404→        self._copy_and_notify(self._output_panel.get_output_text())
   405→
   406→    async def action_dismiss(self, result: object = None) -> None:
   407→        """Dismiss the modal, blocking while command is running.
   408→
   409→        Args:
   410→            result: Optional result to pass to dismiss (unused, for API compat)
   411→        """
   412→        # Block while command is running
   413→        if self._command_running:
   414→            return
   415→
   416→        # If panel exists and completed, refresh data if successful
   417→        if self._output_panel is not None:
   418→            if self._output_panel.is_completed:
   419→                if self._executor and self._output_panel.succeeded:
   420→                    self._executor.refresh_data()
   421→                await self._flush_next_callbacks()
   422→                self.dismiss(result)
   423→            return
   424→
   425→        # Normal dismiss
   426→        await self._flush_next_callbacks()
   427→        self.dismiss(result)
   428→
   429→    def run_streaming_command(
   430→        self,
   431→        command: list[str],
   432→        cwd: Path,
   433→        title: str,
   434→        *,
   435→        timeout: float = 30.0,
   436→        on_success: Callable[[], None] | None = None,
   437→    ) -> None:
   438→        """Run command with live output in bottom panel.
   439→
   440→        Args:
   441→            command: Command to run as list of arguments
   442→            cwd: Working directory for the command
   443→            title: Title to display in the output panel
   444→            timeout: Timeout in seconds (default 30). Set to 0 to disable.
   445→            on_success: Optional callback to invoke on successful completion.
   446→        """
   447→        # Create and mount output panel
   448→        self._output_panel = CommandOutputPanel(title)
   449→        dialog = self.query_one("#detail-dialog")
   450→        dialog.mount(self._output_panel)
   451→        self._command_running = True
   452→        self._on_success_callback = on_success
   453→
   454→        # Set timeout timer if enabled
   455→        if timeout > 0:
   456→            self._stream_timeout_seconds = timeout
   457→            self._stream_timeout_timer = self.set_timer(timeout, self._handle_stream_timeout)
   458→
   459→        # Run subprocess in worker thread
   460→        self._stream_subprocess(command, cwd)
   461→
   462→    def run_close_plan_in_process(
   463→        self,
   464→        plan_id: int,
   465→        plan_url: str,
   466→    ) -> None:
   467→        """Run close plan in-process using HTTP client directly.
   468→
   469→        This is much faster than spawning a subprocess because it uses
   470→        the HTTP client that was initialized at TUI startup.
   471→
   472→        Args:
   473→            plan_id: The plan identifier to close
   474→            plan_url: The plan URL for PR linkage lookup
   475→        """
   476→        # Create and mount output panel
   477→        title = f"Closing Plan #{plan_id}"
   478→        self._output_panel = CommandOutputPanel(title)
   479→        dialog = self.query_one("#detail-dialog")
   480→        dialog.mount(self._output_panel)
   481→        self._command_running = True
   482→
   483→        # Run in-process worker (no timeout needed - HTTP has its own timeout)
   484→        self._run_close_plan_worker(plan_id, plan_url)
   485→
   486→    @work(thread=True)
   487→    def _run_close_plan_worker(
   488→        self,
   489→        plan_id: int,
   490→        plan_url: str,
   491→    ) -> None:
   492→        """Worker: close plan using executor's close_plan_fn.
   493→
   494→        Args:
   495→            plan_id: The plan identifier to close
   496→            plan_url: The plan URL for PR linkage lookup
   497→        """
   498→        panel = self._output_panel
   499→        if panel is None or self._executor is None:
   500→            self._command_running = False
   501→            return
   502→
   503→        success = True
   504→        # Error boundary: catch all exceptions from HTTP operations to display
   505→        # them in the output panel rather than crashing the TUI.
   506→        try:
   507→            self.app.call_from_thread(panel.append_line, "Closing linked PRs...")
   508→            closed_prs = self._executor.close_plan(plan_id, plan_url)
   509→
   510→            if closed_prs:
   511→                pr_list = ", ".join(f"#{pr}" for pr in closed_prs)
   512→                self.app.call_from_thread(panel.append_line, f"Closed PRs: {pr_list}")
   513→
   514→            self.app.call_from_thread(panel.append_line, f"Closed plan #{plan_id}")
   515→        except Exception as e:
   516→            self.app.call_from_thread(panel.append_line, f"Error: {e}")
   517→            success = False
   518→
   519→        self.app.call_from_thread(panel.set_completed, success)
   520→        self._command_running = False
   521→
   522→    def _handle_stream_timeout(self) -> None:
   523→        """Handle timeout of streaming command.
   524→
   525→        Called by Textual timer when the subprocess exceeds the timeout.
   526→        Kills the process and marks the command as failed.
   527→        """
   528→        # Clear timer reference since it has fired
   529→        self._stream_timeout_timer = None
   530→
   531→        # Check if process is still running
   532→        process = self._running_process
   533→        if process is None:
   534→            return
   535→
   536→        # Kill the process if still running (LBYL pattern)
   537→        if process.poll() is None:
   538→            process.kill()
   539→
   540→        # Append timeout message to output panel
   541→        panel = self._output_panel
   542→        if panel is not None:
   543→            panel.append_line("")
   544→            # Format timeout as minutes if >= 60 seconds for readability
   545→            timeout_secs = self._stream_timeout_seconds
   546→            if timeout_secs >= 60:
   547→                minutes = int(timeout_secs // 60)
   548→                timeout_str = f"{minutes} minute{'s' if minutes != 1 else ''}"
   549→            else:
   550→                timeout_str = f"{int(timeout_secs)} seconds"
   551→            panel.append_line(f"⏱️  Command timed out after {timeout_str}")
   552→            panel.set_completed(success=False)
   553→
   554→        # Mark command as no longer running
   555→        self._command_running = False
   556→
   557→    @work(thread=True)
   558→    def _stream_subprocess(self, command: list[str], cwd: Path) -> None:
   559→        """Worker: stream subprocess output to panel.
   560→
   561→        Args:
   562→            command: Command to run
   563→            cwd: Working directory
   564→        """
   565→        # Capture panel reference at start (won't be None since run_streaming_command sets it)
   566→        panel = self._output_panel
   567→        if panel is None:
   568→            self._command_running = False
   569→            return
   570→
   571→        process = subprocess.Popen(
   572→            command,
   573→            cwd=cwd,
   574→            stdout=subprocess.PIPE,
   575→            stderr=subprocess.STDOUT,
   576→            stdin=subprocess.DEVNULL,
   577→            text=True,
   578→            bufsize=1,
   579→        )
   580→
   581→        # Store process reference for timeout handler
   582→        self._running_process = process
   583→
   584→        if process.stdout is not None:
   585→            for line in process.stdout:
   586→                self.app.call_from_thread(
   587→                    panel.append_line,
   588→                    line.rstrip(),
   589→                )
   590→
   591→        return_code = process.wait()
   592→
   593→        # Clear process reference
   594→        self._running_process = None
   595→
   596→        # Cancel timeout timer if still active
   597→        timer = self._stream_timeout_timer
   598→        if timer is not None:
   599→            timer.stop()
   600→            self._stream_timeout_timer = None
   601→
   602→        # Only mark complete if timeout handler hasn't already done so
   603→        if self._command_running:
   604→            success = return_code == 0
   605→            self.app.call_from_thread(panel.set_completed, success)
   606→            self._command_running = False
   607→            # Invoke success callback if provided and command succeeded
   608→            if success:
   609→                callback = self._on_success_callback
   610→                if callback is not None:
   611→                    self.app.call_from_thread(callback)
   612→
   613→    def execute_command(self, command_id: str) -> None:
   614→        """Execute a command from the palette.
   615→
   616→        Args:
   617→            command_id: The ID of the command to execute
   618→        """
   619→        # Import here to avoid circular import at module level
   620→        from erk.tui.app import ErkDashApp
   621→
   622→        if self._executor is None:
   623→            return
   624→
   625→        row = self._row
   626→        executor = self._executor
   627→
   628→        if command_id == "open_browser":
   629→            url = row.pr_url or row.plan_url
   630→            if url:
   631→                executor.open_url(url)
   632→                executor.notify(f"Opened {url}", severity=None)
   633→
   634→        elif command_id == "open_issue":
   635→            if row.plan_url:
   636→                executor.open_url(row.plan_url)
   637→                executor.notify(f"Opened plan #{row.plan_id}", severity=None)
   638→
   639→        elif command_id == "open_pr":
   640→            if row.pr_url:
   641→                executor.open_url(row.pr_url)
   642→                executor.notify(f"Opened PR #{row.pr_number}", severity=None)
   643→
   644→        elif command_id == "open_run":
   645→            if row.run_url:
   646→                executor.open_url(row.run_url)
   647→                executor.notify(f"Opened run {row.run_id_display}", severity=None)
   648→
   649→        elif command_id == "copy_checkout":
   650→            if row.worktree_branch is None:
   651→                executor.notify(
   652→                    "No branch associated with this plan is checked out in a local worktree",
   653→                    severity="warning",
   654→                )
   655→                return
   656→            cmd = f"erk br co {row.worktree_branch}"
   657→            executor.copy_to_clipboard(cmd)
   658→            executor.notify(f"Copied: {cmd}", severity=None)
   659→
   660→        elif command_id == "copy_pr_checkout":
   661→            text = get_copy_text(command_id, row, self._view_mode)
   662→            if text is not None:
   663→                executor.copy_to_clipboard(text)
   664→                executor.notify(f"Copied: {text}", severity=None)
   665→
   666→        elif command_id == "copy_prepare":
   667→            cmd = f"erk br co --for-plan {row.plan_id}"
   668→            executor.copy_to_clipboard(cmd)
   669→            executor.notify(f"Copied: {cmd}", severity=None)
   670→
   671→        elif command_id == "copy_prepare_activate":
   672→            cmd = (
   673→                f'source "$(erk br co --for-plan {row.plan_id} --script)"'
   674→                " && erk implement --dangerous"
   675→            )
   676→            executor.copy_to_clipboard(cmd)
   677→            executor.notify(f"Copied: {cmd}", severity=None)
   678→
   679→        elif command_id == "copy_implement_local":
   680→            text = get_copy_text(command_id, row, self._view_mode)
   681→            if text is not None:
   682→                executor.copy_to_clipboard(text)
   683→                executor.notify(f"Copied: {text}", severity=None)
   684→
   685→        elif command_id == "copy_dispatch":
   686→            text = get_copy_text(command_id, row, self._view_mode)
   687→            if text is not None:
   688→                executor.copy_to_clipboard(text)
   689→                executor.notify(f"Copied: {text}", severity=None)
   690→
   691→        elif command_id == "copy_replan":
   692→            text = get_copy_text(command_id, row, self._view_mode)
   693→            if text is not None:
   694→                executor.copy_to_clipboard(text)
   695→                executor.notify(f"Copied: {text}", severity=None)
   696→
   697→        elif command_id == "copy_land":
   698→            text = get_copy_text(command_id, row, self._view_mode)
   699→            if text is not None:
   700→                executor.copy_to_clipboard(text)
   701→                executor.notify(f"Copied: {text}", severity=None)
   702→
   703→        elif command_id == "copy_close_plan":
   704→            text = get_copy_text(command_id, row, self._view_mode)
   705→            if text is not None:
   706→                executor.copy_to_clipboard(text)
   707→                executor.notify(f"Copied: {text}", severity=None)
   708→
   709→        elif command_id == "copy_rebase_remote":
   710→            text = get_copy_text(command_id, row, self._view_mode)
   711→            if text is not None:
   712→                executor.copy_to_clipboard(text)
   713→                executor.notify(f"Copied: {text}", severity=None)
   714→
   715→        elif command_id == "copy_address_remote":
   716→            text = get_copy_text(command_id, row, self._view_mode)
   717→            if text is not None:
   718→                executor.copy_to_clipboard(text)
   719→                executor.notify(f"Copied: {text}", severity=None)
   720→
   721→        elif command_id == "copy_rewrite_remote":
   722→            if row.pr_number is not None:
   723→                cmd = f"erk launch pr-rewrite --pr {row.pr_number}"
   724→                executor.copy_to_clipboard(cmd)
   725→                executor.notify(f"Copied: {cmd}", severity=None)
   726→
   727→        elif command_id == "rebase_remote":
   728→            if row.pr_number is not None:
   729→                self.dismiss()
   730→                if isinstance(self.app, ErkDashApp):
   731→                    op_id = f"rebase-pr-{row.pr_number}"
   732→                    self.app.notify(f"Dispatching rebase for PR #{row.pr_number}...")
   733→                    self.app._start_operation(
   734→                        op_id=op_id,
   735→                        label=f"Dispatching rebase for PR #{row.pr_number}...",
   736→                    )
   737→                    self.app._rebase_remote_async(op_id, row.pr_number)
   738→
   739→        elif command_id == "address_remote":
   740→            if row.pr_number is not None and self._repo_root is not None:
   741→                self.run_streaming_command(
   742→                    ["erk", "launch", "pr-address", "--pr", str(row.pr_number)],
   743→                    cwd=self._repo_root,
   744→                    title=f"Address Remote PR #{row.pr_number}",
   745→                )
   746→
   747→        elif command_id == "rewrite_remote":
   748→            if row.pr_number is not None:
   749→                self.dismiss()
   750→                if isinstance(self.app, ErkDashApp):
   751→                    self.app.notify(f"Dispatching rewrite for PR #{row.pr_number}...")
   752→                    op_id = f"rewrite-pr-{row.pr_number}"
   753→                    self.app._start_operation(
   754→                        op_id=op_id,
   755→                        label=f"Dispatching rewrite for PR #{row.pr_number}...",
   756→                    )
   757→                    self.app._rewrite_remote_async(op_id, row.pr_number)
   758→
   759→        elif command_id == "close_plan":
   760→            if row.plan_url:
   761→                # Dismiss detail screen first, then run async close on main app
   762→                self.dismiss()
   763→                # Access parent app and trigger async close with toast
   764→                if isinstance(self.app, ErkDashApp):
   765→                    op_id = f"close-plan-{row.plan_id}"
   766→                    self.app._start_operation(op_id=op_id, label=f"Closing plan #{row.plan_id}...")
   767→                    self.app._close_plan_async(op_id, row.plan_id, row.plan_url)
   768→
   769→        elif command_id == "dispatch_to_queue":
   770→            if row.plan_url:
   771→                self.dismiss()
   772→                if isinstance(self.app, ErkDashApp):
   773→                    op_id = f"dispatch-plan-{row.plan_id}"
   774→                    self.app._start_operation(
   775→                        op_id=op_id, label=f"Dispatching plan #{row.plan_id} to queue..."
   776→                    )
   777→                    self.app._dispatch_to_queue_async(op_id, row.plan_id)
   778→
   779→        elif command_id == "land_pr":
   780→            if row.pr_number and row.pr_head_branch:
   781→                self.dismiss()
   782→                if isinstance(self.app, ErkDashApp):
   783→                    op_id = f"land-pr-{row.pr_number}"
   784→                    self.app._start_operation(op_id=op_id, label=f"Landing PR #{row.pr_number}...")
   785→                    self.app._land_pr_async(
   786→                        op_id,
   787→                        row.pr_number,
   788→                        row.pr_head_branch,
   789→                        row.objective_issue,
   790→                    )
   791→
   792→    def compose(self) -> ComposeResult:
   793→        """Create detail dialog content as an Action Hub."""
   794→        with Vertical(id="detail-dialog"):
   795→            # Header: Plan number + title
   796→            with Vertical(id="detail-header"):
   797→                plan_text = f"Plan #{self._row.plan_id}"
   798→                yield Label(plan_text, id="detail-plan-link")
   799→                yield Label(self._row.full_title, id="detail-title", markup=False)
   800→
   801→            # Divider
   802→            yield Label("", id="detail-divider")
   803→
   804→            # PLAN/PR INFO SECTION
   805→            # Plan Info - clickable plan number
   806→            with Container(classes="info-row"):
   807→                yield Label("Plan", classes="info-label")
   808→                if self._row.plan_url:
   809→                    yield ClickableLink(
   810→                        f"#{self._row.plan_id}", self._row.plan_url, classes="info-value"
   811→                    )
   812→                else:
   813→                    yield Label(f"#{self._row.plan_id}", classes="info-value", markup=False)
   814→
   815→            # PR Info (if exists) - clickable PR number with state badge inline
   816→            if self._row.pr_number:
   817→                with Container(classes="info-row"):
   818→                    yield Label("PR", classes="info-label")
   819→                    if self._row.pr_url:
   820→                        yield ClickableLink(
   821→                            f"#{self._row.pr_number}", self._row.pr_url, classes="info-value"
   822→                        )
   823→                    else:
   824→                        yield Label(f"#{self._row.pr_number}", classes="info-value", markup=False)
   825→                    # PR state badge inline
   826→                    pr_text, pr_class = self._get_pr_state_badge()
   827→                    yield Label(pr_text, classes=f"status-badge {pr_class}")
   828→
   829→                # PR title if different from issue title
   830→                if self._row.pr_title and self._row.pr_title != self._row.full_title:
   831→                    with Container(classes="info-row"):
   832→                        yield Label("PR Title", classes="info-label")
   833→                        yield Label(self._row.pr_title, classes="info-value", markup=False)
   834→
   835→                # Checks status
   836→                if self._row.checks_display and self._row.checks_display != "-":
   837→                    with Container(classes="info-row"):
   838→                        yield Label("Checks", classes="info-label")
   839→                        yield Label(self._row.checks_display, classes="info-value", markup=False)
   840→
   841→            # Learn status - always show for visibility into learn workflow
   842→            with Container(classes="info-row"):
   843→                yield Label("Learn", classes="info-label")
   844→                # Make clickable if there's a plan issue, PR, or workflow run
   845→                if self._row.learn_plan_pr is not None and self._row.plan_url:
   846→                    base_url = _extract_repo_base_url(self._row.plan_url)
   847→                    pr_url = f"{base_url}/pull/{self._row.learn_plan_pr}"
   848→                    yield ClickableLink(self._row.learn_display, pr_url, classes="info-value")
   849→                elif self._row.learn_plan_issue is not None and self._row.plan_url:
   850→                    base_url = _extract_repo_base_url(self._row.plan_url)
   851→                    issue_url = f"{base_url}/issues/{self._row.learn_plan_issue}"
   852→                    yield ClickableLink(self._row.learn_display, issue_url, classes="info-value")
   853→                elif self._row.learn_run_url is not None:
   854→                    yield ClickableLink(
   855→                        self._row.learn_display, self._row.learn_run_url, classes="info-value"
   856→                    )
   857→                else:
   858→                    yield Label(self._row.learn_display, classes="info-value", markup=False)
   859→
   860→            # Objective info (if linked)
   861→            if self._row.objective_issue is not None:
   862→                with Container(classes="info-row"):
   863→                    yield Label("Objective", classes="info-label")
   864→                    display_text = (
   865→                        f"{self._row.objective_display} ({self._row.objective_progress_display})"
   866→                        if self._row.objective_progress_display != "-"
   867→                        else self._row.objective_display
   868→                    )
   869→                    if self._row.plan_url:
   870→                        objective_url = (
   871→                            _extract_repo_base_url(self._row.plan_url)
   872→                            + f"/issues/{self._row.objective_issue}"
   873→                        )
   874→                        yield ClickableLink(display_text, objective_url, classes="info-value")
   875→                    else:
   876→                        yield Label(display_text, classes="info-value", markup=False)
   877→
   878→            # REMOTE RUN INFO SECTION (separate from worktree/local info)
   879→            if self._row.run_id:
   880→                with Container(classes="info-row"):
   881→                    yield Label("Run", classes="info-label")
   882→                    if self._row.run_url:
   883→                        yield ClickableLink(
   884→                            self._row.run_id, self._row.run_url, classes="info-value"
   885→                        )
   886→                    else:
   887→                        yield Label(self._row.run_id, classes="info-value", markup=False)
   888→                    # Run status badge inline
   889→                    run_text, run_class = self._get_run_badge()
   890→                    yield Label(run_text, classes=f"status-badge {run_class}")
   891→
   892→                if self._row.remote_impl_display and self._row.remote_impl_display != "-":
   893→                    with Container(classes="info-row"):
   894→                        yield Label("Last remote impl", classes="info-label")
   895→                        yield Label(
   896→                            self._row.remote_impl_display, classes="info-value", markup=False
   897→                        )
   898→
   899→            # COMMANDS SECTION (copy to clipboard)
   900→            # All items below use uniform orange labels that copy when clicked
   901→            yield Label("COMMANDS (copy)", classes="section-header")
   902→
   903→            # PR checkout command (if PR exists)
   904→            if self._row.pr_number is not None:
   905→                pr_checkout_cmd = get_copy_text("copy_pr_checkout", self._row, self._view_mode)
   906→                if pr_checkout_cmd:
   907→                    with Container(classes="command-row"):
   908→                        yield CopyableLabel(pr_checkout_cmd, pr_checkout_cmd)
   909→
   910→            # Prepare commands
   911→            prepare_cmd = f"erk br co --for-plan {self._row.plan_id}"
   912→            with Container(classes="command-row"):
   913→                yield Label("[1]", classes="command-key")
   914→                yield CopyableLabel(prepare_cmd, prepare_cmd)
   915→
   916→            # Submit command
   917→            dispatch_cmd = f"erk pr dispatch {self._row.plan_id}"
   918→            with Container(classes="command-row"):
   919→                yield Label("[3]", classes="command-key")
   920→                yield CopyableLabel(dispatch_cmd, dispatch_cmd)
   921→
   922→            # Log entries (if any) - clickable timestamps
   923→            if self._row.log_entries:
   924→                with Vertical(classes="log-section"):
   925→                    yield Label("Recent activity", classes="log-header")
   926→                    for event_name, timestamp, comment_url in self._row.log_entries[:5]:
   927→                        log_text = f"{timestamp}  {event_name}"
   928→                        if comment_url:
   929→                            yield ClickableLink(log_text, comment_url, classes="log-entry")
   930→                        else:
   931→                            yield Label(log_text, classes="log-entry", markup=False)
   932→
   933→            yield Label("Ctrl+P: commands  Esc: close", id="detail-footer")
   934→

1→"""Command to list plans with filtering."""
     2→

     8→from rich.table import Table

    37→def format_pr_cell(pr: PullRequestInfo, *, use_graphite: bool, graphite_url: str | None) -> str:
    38→    """Format PR cell with clickable link and emoji: #123 👀 or #123 👀🔗
    39→
    40→    The 🔗 emoji is appended for PRs that will auto-close the linked issue when merged.
    41→
    42→    Args:
    43→        pr: PR information
    44→        use_graphite: If True, use Graphite URL; if False, use GitHub URL
    45→        graphite_url: Graphite URL for the PR (None if unavailable)
    46→
    47→    Returns:
    48→        Formatted string for table cell with OSC 8 hyperlink
    49→    """
    50→    emoji = get_pr_status_emoji(pr)
    51→    pr_text = f"#{pr.number}"
    52→
    53→    # Append 🔗 for PRs that will close the issue when merged
    54→    if pr.will_close_target:
    55→        emoji += "🔗"
    56→
    57→    # Determine which URL to use
    58→    url = graphite_url if use_graphite else pr.url
    59→
    60→    # Make PR number clickable if URL is available
    61→    # Rich supports OSC 8 via [link=...] markup
    62→    if url:
    63→        return f"[link={url}]{pr_text}[/link] {emoji}"
    64→    else:
    65→        return f"{pr_text} {emoji}"
    66→

    68→def pr_filter_options(f: Callable[P, T]) -> Callable[P, T]:
    69→    """Shared filter options for pr list commands."""
    70→    f = click.option(
    71→        "--label",
    72→        multiple=True,
    73→        help="Filter by label (can be specified multiple times for AND logic)",
    74→    )(f)
    75→    f = click.option(
    76→        "--state",
    77→        type=click.Choice(["open", "closed"], case_sensitive=False),
    78→        help="Filter by state",
    79→    )(f)
    80→    f = click.option(
    81→        "--run-state",
    82→        type=click.Choice(
    83→            ["queued", "in_progress", "success", "failure", "cancelled"], case_sensitive=False
    84→        ),
    85→        help="Filter by workflow run state",
    86→    )(f)
    87→    f = click.option(
    88→        "--stage",
    89→        type=click.Choice(
    90→            ["prompted", "planning", "planned", "impl", "merged", "closed"],
    91→            case_sensitive=False,
    92→        ),
    93→        help="Filter by lifecycle stage",
    94→    )(f)
    95→    f = click.option(
    96→        "--limit",
    97→        type=int,
    98→        help="Maximum number of results to return",
    99→    )(f)
   100→    f = click.option(
   101→        "--all-users",
   102→        "-A",
   103→        is_flag=True,
   104→        default=False,
   105→        help="Show plans from all users (default: show only your plans)",
   106→    )(f)
   107→    f = click.option(
   108→        "--sort",
   109→        type=click.Choice(["plan", "activity"], case_sensitive=False),
   110→        default="plan",
   111→        help="Sort order: by plan number (default) or recent branch activity",
   112→    )(f)
   113→    return f
   114→

   120→        type=float,
   121→        default=15.0,
   122→        help="Refresh interval in seconds (default: 15.0)",
   123→    )(f)

   127→def _build_static_table(
   128→    rows: list[PlanRowData],
   129→    *,
   130→    show_pr_column: bool,
   131→) -> Table:
   132→    """Build a Rich static table from PlanRowData rows.
   133→
   134→    Column order matches the TUI PlanDataTable exactly.
   135→
   136→    Args:
   137→        rows: List of PlanRowData to display
   138→        show_pr_column: Whether to show a separate PR column
   139→
   140→    Returns:
   141→        Rich Table populated with rows
   142→    """
   143→    table = Table(show_header=True, header_style="bold")
   144→
   145→    # Column setup mirrors PlanDataTable._setup_columns()
   146→    table.add_column("pr", style="cyan", no_wrap=True, width=6)
   147→    table.add_column("stage", no_wrap=True, width=8)
   148→    table.add_column("sts", no_wrap=True, width=4)
   149→    table.add_column("created", no_wrap=True, width=7)
   150→    table.add_column("obj", no_wrap=True, width=5)
   151→    table.add_column("loc", no_wrap=True, width=3)
   152→    table.add_column("branch", no_wrap=True, width=42)
   153→    table.add_column("run-id", no_wrap=True, width=10)
   154→    table.add_column("run", no_wrap=True, width=3)
   155→    table.add_column("author", no_wrap=True, width=9)
   156→    if show_pr_column:
   157→        table.add_column("pr", no_wrap=True, width=10)
   158→    table.add_column("chks", no_wrap=True, width=8)
   159→    table.add_column("cmts", no_wrap=True, width=5)
   160→
   161→    for row in rows:
   162→        values = _row_to_static_values(row, show_pr_column=show_pr_column)
   163→        table.add_row(*values)
   164→
   165→    return table
   166→
   167→
   168→def _row_to_static_values(
   169→    row: PlanRowData,
   170→    *,
   171→    show_pr_column: bool,
   172→) -> tuple[str | Text, ...]:
   173→    """Convert PlanRowData to static table cell values.
   174→
   175→    Mirrors PlanDataTable._row_to_values() for Rich Console rendering.
   176→
   177→    Args:
   178→        row: Plan row data
   179→        show_pr_column: Whether to show a separate PR column
   180→
   181→    Returns:
   182→        Tuple of cell values matching column order
   183→    """
   184→    # Format plan/PR number
   185→    plan_cell: str | Text = f"#{row.plan_id}"
   186→    if row.plan_url:
   187→        plan_cell = f"[link={row.plan_url}]#{row.plan_id}[/link]"
   188→
   189→    # Format objective cell
   190→    objective_cell: str | Text = row.objective_display
   191→    if row.objective_issue is not None and row.objective_url is not None:
   192→        objective_cell = f"[link={row.objective_url}]{row.objective_display}[/link]"
   193→    elif row.objective_issue is not None:
   194→        objective_cell = Text(row.objective_display, style="cyan")
   195→
   196→    # Compact location emoji
   197→    location_parts: list[str] = []
   198→    if row.exists_locally:
   199→        location_parts.append("\U0001f4bb")
   200→    if row.run_url is not None:
   201→        location_parts.append("\u2601")
   202→    location_cell = "".join(location_parts) if location_parts else "-"
   203→
   204→    # run-id and run-state
   205→    run_id: str | Text = strip_rich_markup(row.run_id_display)
   206→    if row.run_url:
   207→        run_id = f"[link={row.run_url}]{run_id}[/link]"
   208→    run_state_text = strip_rich_markup(row.run_state_display)
   209→    run_state_emoji = run_state_text.split(" ", 1)[0] if run_state_text.strip() else "-"
   210→
   211→    # Build values list matching column order
   212→    stage_display = strip_rich_markup(row.lifecycle_display)
   213→    values: list[str | Text] = [
   214→        plan_cell,
   215→        stage_display,
   216→        row.status_display,
   217→        row.created_display,
   218→        objective_cell,
   219→        location_cell,
   220→        row.pr_head_branch or row.worktree_branch or "-",
   221→        run_id,
   222→        run_state_emoji,
   223→    ]
   224→    values.append(row.author)
   225→
   226→    checks_display = strip_rich_markup(row.checks_display)
   227→    comments_display = strip_rich_markup(row.comments_display)
   228→    if show_pr_column:
   229→        pr_display = strip_rich_markup(row.pr_display)
   230→        if row.pr_url:
   231→            pr_display = f"[link={row.pr_url}]{pr_display}[/link]"
   232→        values.extend([pr_display, checks_display, comments_display])
   233→    else:
   234→        values.extend([checks_display, comments_display])
   235→    return tuple(values)
   236→
   237→
   238→def _pr_list_impl(
   239→    ctx: ErkContext,
   240→    *,
   241→    label: tuple[str, ...],
   242→    state: str | None,
   243→    run_state: str | None,
   244→    stage: str | None,
   245→    limit: int | None,
   246→    all_users: bool,
   247→    sort: str,
   248→) -> None:
   249→    """Implementation logic for listing plans with optional filters.
   250→
   251→    Uses RealPlanDataProvider to fetch data (same as erk dash TUI),
   252→    then renders as a static Rich table matching the TUI column layout.
   253→    """
   254→    repo = discover_repo_context(ctx, ctx.cwd)
   255→    ensure_erk_metadata_dir(repo)
   256→    repo_root = repo.root
   257→
   258→    if repo.github is None:
   259→        user_output(click.style("Error: ", fg="red") + "Could not determine repository owner/name")
   260→        raise SystemExit(1)
   261→    owner = repo.github.owner
   262→    repo_name = repo.github.repo
   263→
   264→    # Determine creator filter
   265→    creator: str | None = None
   266→    if not all_users:
   267→        is_authenticated, username, _ = ctx.github.check_auth_status()
   268→        if is_authenticated and username:
   269→            creator = username
   270→
   271→    # Build labels - default to ["erk-plan"]
   272→    labels = label if label else ("erk-pr", "erk-plan")
   273→
   274→    # Construct RealPlanDataProvider
   275→    location = GitHubRepoLocation(root=repo_root, repo_id=GitHubRepoId(owner, repo_name))
   276→
   277→    http_client = ctx.http_client
   278→    if http_client is None:
   279→        user_output(click.style("Error: ", fg="red") + "GitHub authentication not available")
   280→        raise SystemExit(1)
   281→
   282→    provider = RealPlanDataProvider(
   283→        ctx,
   284→        location=location,
   285→        clipboard=FakeClipboard(),
   286→        browser=FakeBrowserLauncher(),
   287→        http_client=http_client,
   288→    )
   289→
   290→    effective_state: IssueFilterState = "closed" if state == "closed" else "open"
   291→
   292→    filters = PlanFilters(
   293→        labels=labels,
   294→        state=effective_state,
   295→        run_state=run_state,
   296→        limit=limit,
   297→        show_prs=True,
   298→        show_runs=True,
   299→        exclude_labels=(),
   300→        creator=creator,
   301→        show_pr_column=False,
   302→        lifecycle_stage=stage,
   303→    )
   304→
   305→    # Fetch data via provider
   306→    rows, timings = provider.fetch_plans(filters)
   307→
   308→    # Display warnings about degraded data quality
   309→    if timings is not None and timings.warnings:
   310→        for warning in timings.warnings:
   311→            user_output(click.style("Warning: ", fg="yellow") + warning)
   312→
   313→    # Apply --stage post-fetch filtering
   314→    if stage is not None:
   315→        rows = [r for r in rows if strip_rich_markup(r.lifecycle_display).startswith(stage)]
   316→
   317→    if not rows:
   318→        user_output("No plans found matching the criteria.")
   319→        return
   320→
   321→    # Sort rows
   322→    sort_key = SortKey.BRANCH_ACTIVITY if sort == "activity" else SortKey.PLAN_ID
   323→    if sort_key == SortKey.BRANCH_ACTIVITY:
   324→        activity_by_plan = provider.fetch_branch_activity(rows)
   325→        rows = sort_plans(rows, sort_key, activity_by_plan=activity_by_plan)
   326→    else:
   327→        rows = sort_plans(rows, sort_key)
   328→
   329→    # Build and display static table
   330→    table = _build_static_table(
   331→        rows,
   332→        show_pr_column=False,
   333→    )
   334→
   335→    user_output(f"\nFound {len(rows)} plan(s):\n")
   336→    console = Console(stderr=True, width=200, force_terminal=True)
   337→    console.print(table)
   338→    console.print()
   339→
   340→
   341→def _run_interactive_mode(
   342→    ctx: ErkContext,
   343→    *,
   344→    label: tuple[str, ...],
   345→    state: str | None,
   346→    run_state: str | None,
   347→    stage: str | None,
   348→    runs: bool,
   349→    prs: bool,
   350→    limit: int | None,
   351→    interval: float,
   352→    all_users: bool,
   353→    sort: str,
   354→) -> None:
   355→    """Run interactive TUI mode.
   356→
   357→    Args:
   358→        ctx: ErkContext with all dependencies
   359→        label: Labels to filter by

"""Command to list plans with filtering."""

from collections.abc import Callable
from typing import ParamSpec, TypeVar

import click
from rich.console import Console
from rich.table import Table
from rich.text import Text

from erk.cli.core import discover_repo_context
from erk.core.context import ErkContext
from erk.core.display_utils import strip_rich_markup
from erk.core.repo_discovery import ensure_erk_metadata_dir
from erk.tui.app import ErkDashApp
from erk.tui.data.types import PlanFilters, PlanRowData
from erk.tui.sorting.logic import sort_plans
from erk.tui.sorting.types import SortKey, SortState
from erk_shared.gateway.browser.fake import FakeBrowserLauncher
from erk_shared.gateway.browser.real import RealBrowserLauncher
from erk_shared.gateway.clipboard.fake import FakeClipboard
from erk_shared.gateway.clipboard.real import RealClipboard
from erk_shared.gateway.github.emoji import get_pr_status_emoji
from erk_shared.gateway.github.types import (
    GitHubRepoId,
    GitHubRepoLocation,
    IssueFilterState,
    PullRequestInfo,
)
from erk_shared.gateway.plan_data_provider.real import RealPlanDataProvider
from erk_shared.output.output import user_output

P = ParamSpec("P")
T = TypeVar("T")


def format_pr_cell(pr: PullRequestInfo, *, use_graphite: bool, graphite_url: str | None) -> str:
    """Format PR cell with clickable link and emoji: #123 👀 or #123 👀🔗

    The 🔗 emoji is appended for PRs that will auto-close the linked issue when merged.

    Args:
        pr: PR information
        use_graphite: If True, use Graphite URL; if False, use GitHub URL
        graphite_url: Graphite URL for the PR (None if unavailable)

    Returns:
        Formatted string for table cell with OSC 8 hyperlink
    """
    emoji = get_pr_status_emoji(pr)
    pr_text = f"#{pr.number}"

    # Append 🔗 for PRs that will close the issue when merged
    if pr.will_close_target:
        emoji += "🔗"

    # Determine which URL to use
    url = graphite_url if use_graphite else pr.url

    # Make PR number clickable if URL is available
    # Rich supports OSC 8 via [link=...] markup
    if url:
        return f"[link={url}]{pr_text}[/link] {emoji}"
    else:
        return f"{pr_text} {emoji}"


def pr_filter_options(f: Callable[P, T]) -> Callable[P, T]:
    """Shared filter options for pr list commands."""
    f = click.option(
        "--label",
        multiple=True,
        help="Filter by label (can be specified multiple times for AND logic)",
    )(f)
    f = click.option(
        "--state",
        type=click.Choice(["open", "closed"], case_sensitive=False),
        help="Filter by state",
    )(f)
    f = click.option(
        "--run-state",
        type=click.Choice(
            ["queued", "in_progress", "success", "failure", "cancelled"], case_sensitive=False
        ),
        help="Filter by workflow run state",
    )(f)
    f = click.option(
        "--stage",
        type=click.Choice(
            ["prompted", "planning", "planned", "impl", "merged", "closed"],
            case_sensitive=False,
        ),
        help="Filter by lifecycle stage",
    )(f)
    f = click.option(
        "--limit",
        type=int,
        help="Maximum number of results to return",
    )(f)
    f = click.option(
        "--all-users",
        "-A",
        is_flag=True,
        default=False,
        help="Show plans from all users (default: show only your plans)",
    )(f)
    f = click.option(
        "--sort",
        type=click.Choice(["plan", "activity"], case_sensitive=False),
        default="plan",
        help="Sort order: by plan number (default) or recent branch activity",
    )(f)
    return f


def dash_options(f: Callable[P, T]) -> Callable[P, T]:
    """TUI-specific options for dash command."""
    f = click.option(
        "--interval",
        type=float,
        default=15.0,
        help="Refresh interval in seconds (default: 15.0)",
    )(f)
    return f


def _build_static_table(
    rows: list[PlanRowData],
    *,
    show_pr_column: bool,
) -> Table:
    """Build a Rich static table from PlanRowData rows.

    Column order matches the TUI PlanDataTable exactly.

    Args:
        rows: List of PlanRowData to display
        show_pr_column: Whether to show a separate PR column

    Returns:
        Rich Table populated with rows
    """
    table = Table(show_header=True, header_style="bold")

    # Column setup mirrors PlanDataTable._setup_columns()
    table.add_column("pr", style="cyan", no_wrap=True, width=6)
    table.add_column("stage", no_wrap=True, width=8)
    table.add_column("sts", no_wrap=True, width=4)
    table.add_column("created", no_wrap=True, width=7)
    table.add_column("obj", no_wrap=True, width=5)
    table.add_column("loc", no_wrap=True, width=3)
    table.add_column("branch", no_wrap=True, width=42)
    table.add_column("run-id", no_wrap=True, width=10)
    table.add_column("run", no_wrap=True, width=3)
    table.add_column("author", no_wrap=True, width=9)
    if show_pr_column:
        table.add_column("pr", no_wrap=True, width=10)
    table.add_column("chks", no_wrap=True, width=8)
    table.add_column("cmts", no_wrap=True, width=5)

    for row in rows:
        values = _row_to_static_values(row, show_pr_column=show_pr_column)
        table.add_row(*values)

    return table


def _row_to_static_values(
    row: PlanRowData,
    *,
    show_pr_column: bool,
) -> tuple[str | Text, ...]:
    """Convert PlanRowData to static table cell values.

    Mirrors PlanDataTable._row_to_values() for Rich Console rendering.

    Args:
        row: Plan row data
        show_pr_column: Whether to show a separate PR column

    Returns:
        Tuple of cell values matching column order
    """
    # Format plan/PR number
    plan_cell: str | Text = f"#{row.plan_id}"
    if row.plan_url:
        plan_cell = f"[link={row.plan_url}]#{row.plan_id}[/link]"

    # Format objective cell
    objective_cell: str | Text = row.objective_display
    if row.objective_issue is not None and row.objective_url is not None:
        objective_cell = f"[link={row.objective_url}]{row.objective_display}[/link]"
    elif row.objective_issue is not None:
        objective_cell = Text(row.objective_display, style="cyan")

    # Compact location emoji
    location_parts: list[str] = []
    if row.exists_locally:
        location_parts.append("\U0001f4bb")
    if row.run_url is not None:
        location_parts.append("\u2601")
    location_cell = "".join(location_parts) if location_parts else "-"

    # run-id and run-state
    run_id: str | Text = strip_rich_markup(row.run_id_display)
    if row.run_url:
        run_id = f"[link={row.run_url}]{run_id}[/link]"
    run_state_text = strip_rich_markup(row.run_state_display)
    run_state_emoji = run_state_text.split(" ", 1)[0] if run_state_text.strip() else "-"

    # Build values list matching column order
    stage_display = strip_rich_markup(row.lifecycle_display)
    values: list[str | Text] = [
        plan_cell,
        stage_display,
        row.status_display,
        row.created_display,
        objective_cell,
        location_cell,
        row.pr_head_branch or row.worktree_branch or "-",
        run_id,
        run_state_emoji,
    ]
    values.append(row.author)

    checks_display = strip_rich_markup(row.checks_display)
    comments_display = strip_rich_markup(row.comments_display)
    if show_pr_column:
        pr_display = strip_rich_markup(row.pr_display)
        if row.pr_url:
            pr_display = f"[link={row.pr_url}]{pr_display}[/link]"
        values.extend([pr_display, checks_display, comments_display])
    else:
        values.extend([checks_display, comments_display])
    return tuple(values)


def _pr_list_impl(
    ctx: ErkContext,
    *,
    label: tuple[str, ...],
    state: str | None,
    run_state: str | None,
    stage: str | None,
    limit: int | None,
    all_users: bool,
    sort: str,
) -> None:
    """Implementation logic for listing plans with optional filters.

    Uses RealPlanDataProvider to fetch data (same as erk dash TUI),
    then renders as a static Rich table matching the TUI column layout.
    """
    repo = discover_repo_context(ctx, ctx.cwd)
    ensure_erk_metadata_dir(repo)
    repo_root = repo.root

    if repo.github is None:
        user_output(click.style("Error: ", fg="red") + "Could not determine repository owner/name")
        raise SystemExit(1)
    owner = repo.github.owner
    repo_name = repo.github.repo

    # Determine creator filter
    creator: str | None = None
    if not all_users:
        is_authenticated, username, _ = ctx.github.check_auth_status()
        if is_authenticated and username:
            creator = username

    # Build labels - default to ["erk-plan"]
    labels = label if label else ("erk-pr", "erk-plan")

    # Construct RealPlanDataProvider
    location = GitHubRepoLocation(root=repo_root, repo_id=GitHubRepoId(owner, repo_name))

    http_client = ctx.http_client
    if http_client is None:
        user_output(click.style("Error: ", fg="red") + "GitHub authentication not available")
        raise SystemExit(1)

    provider = RealPlanDataProvider(
        ctx,
        location=location,
        clipboard=FakeClipboard(),
        browser=FakeBrowserLauncher(),
        http_client=http_client,
    )

    effective_state: IssueFilterState = "closed" if state == "closed" else "open"

    filters = PlanFilters(
        labels=labels,
        state=effective_state,
        run_state=run_state,
        limit=limit,
        show_prs=True,
        show_runs=True,
        exclude_labels=(),
        creator=creator,
        show_pr_column=False,
        lifecycle_stage=stage,
    )

    # Fetch data via provider
    rows, timings = provider.fetch_plans(filters)

    # Display warnings about degraded data quality
    if timings is not None and timings.warnings:
        for warning in timings.warnings:
            user_output(click.style("Warning: ", fg="yellow") + warning)

    # Apply --stage post-fetch filtering
    if stage is not None:
        rows = [r for r in rows if strip_rich_markup(r.lifecycle_display).startswith(stage)]

    if not rows:
        user_output("No plans found matching the criteria.")
        return

    # Sort rows
    sort_key = SortKey.BRANCH_ACTIVITY if sort == "activity" else SortKey.PLAN_ID
    if sort_key == SortKey.BRANCH_ACTIVITY:
        activity_by_plan = provider.fetch_branch_activity(rows)
        rows = sort_plans(rows, sort_key, activity_by_plan=activity_by_plan)
    else:
        rows = sort_plans(rows, sort_key)

    # Build and display static table
    table = _build_static_table(
        rows,
        show_pr_column=False,
    )

    user_output(f"\nFound {len(rows)} plan(s):\n")
    console = Console(stderr=True, width=200, force_terminal=True)
    console.print(table)
    console.print()


def _run_interactive_mode(
    ctx: ErkContext,
    *,
    label: tuple[str, ...],
    state: str | None,
    run_state: str | None,
    stage: str | None,
    runs: bool,
    prs: bool,
    limit: int | None,
    interval: float,
    all_users: bool,
    sort: str,
) -> None:
    """Run interactive TUI mode.

    Args:
        ctx: ErkContext with all dependencies
        label: Labels to filter by
        state: State filter ("open", "closed", or None)
        run_state: Workflow run state filter
        stage: Lifecycle stage filter (e.g., "impl", "planned")
        runs: Whether to show run columns
        prs: Whether to show PR columns
        limit: Maximum number of results
        interval: Refresh interval in seconds
        all_users: If True, show plans from all users; if False, filter to authenticated user
        sort: Sort order ("plan" or "activity")
    """
    repo = discover_repo_context(ctx, ctx.cwd)
    ensure_erk_metadata_dir(repo)
    repo_root = repo.root

    # Get owner/repo from RepoContext (already populated via git remote URL parsing)
    if repo.github is None:
        user_output(click.style("Error: ", fg="red") + "Could not determine repository owner/name")
        raise SystemExit(1)
    owner = repo.github.owner
    repo_name = repo.github.repo

    # Determine creator filter: None for all users, authenticated username otherwise
    creator: str | None = None
    if not all_users:
        is_authenticated, username, _ = ctx.github.check_auth_status()
        if is_authenticated and username:
            creator = username

    # Build labels - default to ["erk-plan"]
    labels = label if label else ("erk-pr", "erk-plan")

    # Create data provider and filters
    location = GitHubRepoLocation(root=repo_root, repo_id=GitHubRepoId(owner, repo_name))
    clipboard = RealClipboard()
    browser = RealBrowserLauncher()

    http_client = ctx.http_client
    if http_client is None:
        user_output(click.style("Error: ", fg="red") + "GitHub authentication not available")
        raise SystemExit(1)

    provider = RealPlanDataProvider(
        ctx,
        location=location,
        clipboard=clipboard,
        browser=browser,
        http_client=http_client,
    )
    effective_state: IssueFilterState = "closed" if state == "closed" else "open"

    filters = PlanFilters(
        labels=labels,
        state=effective_state,
        run_state=run_state,
        limit=limit,
        show_prs=prs,
        show_runs=runs,
        exclude_labels=(),
        creator=creator,
        show_pr_column=False,
        lifecycle_stage=stage,
    )

    # Convert sort string to SortState
    initial_sort = SortState(key=SortKey.BRANCH_ACTIVITY if sort == "activity" else SortKey.PLAN_ID)

    # Run the TUI app
    app = ErkDashApp(
        provider=provider,
        filters=filters,
        refresh_interval=interval,
        initial_sort=initial_sort,
    )
    app.run()


@click.command("list")
@pr_filter_options
@click.pass_obj
def pr_list(
    ctx: ErkContext,
    *,
    label: tuple[str, ...],
    state: str | None,
    run_state: str | None,
    stage: str | None,
    limit: int | None,
    all_users: bool,
    sort: str,
) -> None:
    """List plans as a static table.

    By default, shows only plans created by you. Use --all-users (-A)
    to show plans from all users.

    Examples:
        erk pr list                      # Your plans only
        erk pr list --all-users          # All users' plans
        erk pr list -A                   # All users' plans (short form)
        erk pr list --state open
        erk pr list --label erk-plan --label bug
        erk pr list --limit 10
        erk pr list --run-state in_progress
        erk pr list --stage impl         # Filter by lifecycle stage
        erk pr list --sort activity      # Sort by recent branch activity
    """
    _pr_list_impl(
        ctx,
        label=label,
        state=state,
        run_state=run_state,
        stage=stage,
        limit=limit,
        all_users=all_users,
        sort=sort,
    )


@click.command("dash")
@pr_filter_options
@dash_options
@click.pass_obj
def dash(
    ctx: ErkContext,
    *,
    label: tuple[str, ...],
    state: str | None,
    run_state: str | None,
    stage: str | None,
    limit: int | None,
    all_users: bool,
    sort: str,
    interval: float,
) -> None:
    """Interactive plan dashboard (TUI).

    By default, shows only plans created by you. Use --all-users (-A)
    to show plans from all users.

    Launches an interactive terminal UI for viewing and managing plans.
    Shows all columns (runs) by default. For a static table output, use
    'erk pr list' instead.

    Examples:
        erk dash                         # Your plans only
        erk dash --all-users             # All users' plans
        erk dash -A                      # All users' plans (short form)
        erk dash --interval 10
        erk dash --label erk-plan --state open
        erk dash --limit 10
        erk dash --run-state in_progress
        erk dash --stage impl            # Filter by lifecycle stage
        erk dash --sort activity         # Sort by recent branch activity
    """
    # Default to showing all columns (runs=True)
    prs = True  # Always show PRs
    runs = True  # Default to showing runs

    _run_interactive_mode(
        ctx,
        label=label,
        state=state,
        run_state=run_state,
        stage=stage,
        runs=runs,
        prs=prs,
        limit=limit,
        interval=interval,
        all_users=all_users,
        sort=sort,
    )

     4→    get_all_commands,

     9→from erk.tui.commands.types import CommandCategory, CommandContext
    10→from erk.tui.views.types import ViewMode

    13→
    14→def test_all_commands_have_unique_ids() -> None:
    15→    """All commands should have unique IDs."""

    17→    ids = [cmd.id for cmd in commands]
    18→    assert len(ids) == len(set(ids)), "Command IDs must be unique"

    21→def test_all_commands_have_required_fields() -> None:

    25→        assert cmd.id, f"Command missing id: {cmd}"

   494→def test_format_palette_display_produces_styled_text() -> None:
   495→    """_format_palette_display produces Text with correct structure and dim command."""
   496→    from rich.text import Text
   497→
   498→    from erk.tui.commands.provider import _format_palette_display
   499→
   500→    result = _format_palette_display("⚡", "close", "erk pr close 123")
   501→
   502→    # Result should be a Text object
   503→    assert isinstance(result, Text)
   504→
   505→    # Plain text should match expected format
   506→    assert result.plain == "⚡ close: erk pr close 123"
   507→
   508→    # Command portion should be dimmed
   509→    # Check that "dim" style is applied to the command text
   510→    spans = list(result.spans)
   511→    # The structure is: emoji + " ", label + ": ", (command_text, "dim")
   512→    # Find the span covering the command text portion
   513→    command_start = len("⚡ close: ")
   514→    command_span = next((s for s in spans if s.start == command_start), None)
   515→    assert command_span is not None, "Expected span for command text"
   516→    assert command_span.style == "dim"

   527→    highlighted = Text("close: erk pr close 123")
   528→    highlighted.stylize("bold", 0, 5)  # First "close" highlighted
   529→
   530→    result = _format_search_display("⚡", highlighted, len("close"))

   532→    assert isinstance(result, Text)

   713→def test_shortcuts_no_conflicts_within_view() -> None:
   714→    """Shortcuts should not conflict within the same view mode.
   715→
   716→    Plan commands and objective commands can reuse shortcuts (e.g., "i", "s", "1")
   717→    because they are mutually exclusive (filtered by view mode). But within a single
   718→    view, shortcuts must be unique.
   719→    """
   720→    row = make_plan_row(
   721→        123,
   722→        "Test",
   723→        plan_url="https://github.com/test/repo/issues/123",

   729→    )
   730→
   731→    for view_mode in (ViewMode.PLANS, ViewMode.OBJECTIVES):
   732→        ctx = CommandContext(row=row, view_mode=view_mode)
   733→        commands = get_available_commands(ctx)
   734→        shortcuts = [cmd.shortcut for cmd in commands if cmd.shortcut is not None]
   735→        assert len(shortcuts) == len(set(shortcuts)), (

   737→        )

"""Tests for command palette registry."""

from erk.tui.commands.registry import (
    get_all_commands,
    get_available_commands,
    get_copy_text,
    get_display_name,
)
from erk.tui.commands.types import CommandCategory, CommandContext
from erk.tui.views.types import ViewMode
from erk_shared.gateway.plan_data_provider.fake import make_plan_row


def test_all_commands_have_unique_ids() -> None:
    """All commands should have unique IDs."""
    commands = get_all_commands()
    ids = [cmd.id for cmd in commands]
    assert len(ids) == len(set(ids)), "Command IDs must be unique"


def test_all_commands_have_required_fields() -> None:
    """All commands should have required fields populated."""
    commands = get_all_commands()
    for cmd in commands:
        assert cmd.id, f"Command missing id: {cmd}"
        assert cmd.name, f"Command {cmd.id} missing name"
        assert cmd.description, f"Command {cmd.id} missing description"
        assert isinstance(cmd.category, CommandCategory), f"Command {cmd.id} missing valid category"
        assert callable(cmd.is_available), f"Command {cmd.id} missing is_available"


def test_open_issue_available_when_issue_url_exists() -> None:
    """open_issue should be available when issue URL exists."""
    row = make_plan_row(123, "Test", plan_url="https://github.com/test/repo/issues/123")
    ctx = CommandContext(row=row, view_mode=ViewMode.PLANS)
    commands = get_available_commands(ctx)
    cmd_ids = [cmd.id for cmd in commands]
    assert "open_issue" in cmd_ids


def test_open_pr_available_when_pr_url_exists() -> None:
    """open_pr should be available when PR URL exists."""
    row = make_plan_row(123, "Test", pr_url="https://github.com/test/repo/pull/456")
    ctx = CommandContext(row=row, view_mode=ViewMode.PLANS)
    commands = get_available_commands(ctx)
    cmd_ids = [cmd.id for cmd in commands]
    assert "open_pr" in cmd_ids


def test_open_pr_not_available_when_no_pr() -> None:
    """open_pr should not be available when no PR URL."""
    row = make_plan_row(123, "Test")
    ctx = CommandContext(row=row, view_mode=ViewMode.PLANS)
    commands = get_available_commands(ctx)
    cmd_ids = [cmd.id for cmd in commands]
    assert "open_pr" not in cmd_ids


def test_open_run_available_when_run_url_exists() -> None:
    """open_run should be available when run URL exists."""
    row = make_plan_row(123, "Test", run_url="https://github.com/test/repo/actions/runs/789")
    ctx = CommandContext(row=row, view_mode=ViewMode.PLANS)
    commands = get_available_commands(ctx)
    cmd_ids = [cmd.id for cmd in commands]
    assert "open_run" in cmd_ids


def test_open_run_not_available_when_no_run() -> None:
    """open_run should not be available when no run URL."""

def test_copy_replan_available_when_issue_url_exists() -> None:

# === Dynamic Display Name Tests (Plan Commands) ===


def test_display_name_close_plan_shows_cli_command() -> None:
    """close_plan should show the CLI command with issue number."""
    row = make_plan_row(5831, "Test Plan")
    ctx = CommandContext(row=row, view_mode=ViewMode.PLANS)

# === Palette Display Formatting Tests ===


def test_format_palette_display_produces_styled_text() -> None:
    """_format_palette_display produces Text with correct structure and dim command."""
    from rich.text import Text

    from erk.tui.commands.provider import _format_palette_display

    result = _format_palette_display("⚡", "close", "erk pr close 123")

    # Result should be a Text object
    assert isinstance(result, Text)

    # Plain text should match expected format
    assert result.plain == "⚡ close: erk pr close 123"

    # Command portion should be dimmed
    # Check that "dim" style is applied to the command text
    spans = list(result.spans)
    # The structure is: emoji + " ", label + ": ", (command_text, "dim")
    # Find the span covering the command text portion
    command_start = len("⚡ close: ")
    command_span = next((s for s in spans if s.start == command_start), None)
    assert command_span is not None, "Expected span for command text"
    assert command_span.style == "dim"


def test_format_search_display_preserves_highlighting() -> None:
    """_format_search_display preserves fuzzy match highlights in dim portion."""
    from rich.text import Text

    from erk.tui.commands.provider import _format_search_display

    # Simulate highlighted text from fuzzy matcher
    # e.g., "close: erk pr close 123" with "close" highlighted
    highlighted = Text("close: erk pr close 123")
    highlighted.stylize("bold", 0, 5)  # First "close" highlighted

    result = _format_search_display("⚡", highlighted, len("close"))

    assert isinstance(result, Text)
    assert result.plain == "⚡ close: erk pr close 123"

# === Shortcut Safety Tests ===


def test_shortcuts_no_conflicts_within_view() -> None:
    """Shortcuts should not conflict within the same view mode.

    Plan commands and objective commands can reuse shortcuts (e.g., "i", "s", "1")
    because they are mutually exclusive (filtered by view mode). But within a single
    view, shortcuts must be unique.
    """
    row = make_plan_row(
        123,
        "Test",
        plan_url="https://github.com/test/repo/issues/123",
        pr_number=456,
        pr_url="https://github.com/test/repo/pull/456",
        pr_state="OPEN",
        worktree_branch="feature-123",
        run_url="https://github.com/test/repo/actions/runs/789",
    )

    for view_mode in (ViewMode.PLANS, ViewMode.OBJECTIVES):
        ctx = CommandContext(row=row, view_mode=view_mode)
        commands = get_available_commands(ctx)
        shortcuts = [cmd.shortcut for cmd in commands if cmd.shortcut is not None]
        assert len(shortcuts) == len(set(shortcuts)), (
            f"Duplicate shortcuts in {view_mode.name} view: {shortcuts}"
        )

7. Modify `_display_copy_pr_checkout` to conditionally generate cmux command
8. Thread through `ErkDashApp` in `app.py`
9. Thread through `PlanDetailScreen` in `plan_detail_screen.py`
10. Wire from CLI in `list_cmd.py`
11. Add tests

Looking at the code more carefully, I notice that `get_copy_text` creates a `CommandContext` internally, so I need to pass `cmux_integration` into that context when it's instantiated rather than as a separate parameter.

The approach is to add a `cmux_integration` field to `CommandContext`, then pass it through when creating the context in `get_copy_text`, and have `_display_copy_pr_checkout` check that flag to conditionally generate the cmux command. But I'm noticing the project has a strict rule against default parameter values, yet the plan explicitly specifies `cmux_integration: bool = False` — I need to reconcile this with the existing code style. The plan explicitly includes the default value to avoid breaking existing callers of `get_copy_text`, which makes sense even though it conflicts with the project's no-defaults rule—the plan author chose this pragmatically to maintain backward compatibility.
